mnemonics: FETCH-DECODE-EXECUTE: The fundamental instruction cycle, like a chef getting a recipe (fetch), reading it (decode), and cooking (execute).
keyPoints: The Memory Address Register (MAR) holds the address of the word to be accessed in memory.
The Memory Data Register (MDR) holds the data word that is read from or written to the memory.
Storing a word requires the CPU to send the data to store (MDR) and the location to store it (MAR) to the memory unit, which then performs a write operation.
commonMistakes: Confusing the roles of MAR (holds the address) and MDR (holds the data).
Assuming the entire instruction is processed in a single clock cycle; it requires multiple cycles (fetch, decode, execute).
